title: Commercial Vehicle Driver
description: A commercial vehicle driver in India is responsible for transporting goods and people across the country. They drive long hours, often in challenging weather conditions, and are required to maintain the vehicle's condition and adhere to traffic rules. They also handle paperwork and interact with customers to ensure smooth delivery of goods or passengers.
top_private_colleges: Maharaja Sayajirao University of Baroda, Vadodara
Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology, Patiala

career_progression.year_0_2: Start as a trainee driver, gain experience and obtain a commercial driving license.
career_progression.year_3_5: Get promoted to a senior driver position, take on more responsibilities and handle complex routes.
career_progression.year_6_10: Become a fleet manager or a transport coordinator, oversee multiple drivers and vehicles.
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Start a transportation company or a logistics firm, hire and manage drivers.
career_progression.global_pathway: Opportunities for Indians in this career are limited abroad, but can be found in countries with high demand for transportation services.

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Starting as a driver can be challenging, with long hours and low pay.
common_misconceptions: Drivers are not required to have a degree.
